mission: Design (not implement) a record-bound, subject-bound, semantically-typed availability-evidence schema v2 that proves one exact archived source record binds one locked source identity to one field family to the exact mirrored value and to a typed public-availability timestamp. Prohibits document-wide substring co-occurrence, reviewer prose, self-declared timestamps, event/retrieval/generation/ingestion timestamps, generic archive existence, and name-only/fuzzy subject matching.

source_inventory.cross_cutting_finding: The six upstream files compute_rookie_transition_profile.py reads are TIBER-Rookies' own internal pipeline artifacts, not the original public record for most fields; v2 evidence packages must generally reach past them to the actual original public source.

draft_capital.direct_or_derived: direct pass-through of the score (verified byte-identical across all 48 rows); big_board_rank is a direct player_id lookup
draft_capital.source_record_grain: one row per player_id, 101 rows (data/processed/{season}_draft_capital_proxy.json), broader than the 48-player modeled population
draft_capital.rounding_normalization: documented rank-band conversion through 76-100=45 (docs/export-contract.md:152-168); the 101-150/151+ bands exist only in code -- UNCERTAIN, not found documented elsewhere
draft_capital.missing_evidence: a real, archived, original big-board publication naming this player at this rank, dated

age_at_entry.mirror_value_shape: integer age, or null with source_type unavailable
age_at_entry.direct_or_derived: derived deterministically from dob via age_from_dob(dob, season)
age_at_entry.derivation_formula: season_start = date(season, 9, 1); age = season_start.year - born.year - (1 if (season_start.month, season_start.day) < (born.month, born.day) else 0)
age_at_entry.source_record_grain: one row per player_id, 100 rows, broader than the 48-player modeled population
age_at_entry.derivation_code_path: build_age_field(), scripts/compute_rookie_transition_profile.py:175-195; formula in age_from_dob(), lines 68-80 (verbatim copy of scripts/compute_breakout_age.py)
age_at_entry.rounding_normalization: none on the age integer; exact calendar arithmetic
age_at_entry.cohort_dependency: none on the computation, but dob's own real provenance is heterogeneous and largely undocumented per-row: 17/48 players sourced from an undocumented 'manual_seed_2026' label (reliability UNCERTAIN), only 1/48 from an actual identifiable public source (2026 NFL Combine results, ESPN/NFL.com)
age_at_entry.missing_evidence: a real, archived, original public record stating the DOB (e.g. a combine bio) with its own genuine publish date

athletic_testing.mirror_value_shape: { athletic_score_0_100: number, athletic_source: string }, or null/unavailable when Rookie Alpha's athletic_source is absent or the NEUTRAL_DEFAULT placeholder
athletic_testing.direct_or_derived: pass-through from Rookie Alpha's scores block, with NEUTRAL_DEFAULT (a placeholder default of 50.0) intentionally converted to unavailable rather than copied
upstream_files: exports/promoted/rookie-alpha/{season}_rookie_alpha_predraft_v0.json (scores block only -- not one of the six nominal files); ultimate raw source data/raw/{season}_combine_results.json is two hops upstream and never read directly
athletic_testing.source_record_grain: Rookie Alpha players[], one row per player_id, matches the 48-player population exactly
athletic_testing.derivation_code_path: build_athletic_testing_field(), scripts/compute_rookie_transition_profile.py:198-229; real formula owned by scripts/compute_rookie_alpha.py (outside the six inventoried files)
athletic_testing.derivation_formula: within-class z-score: 50 + 16.6667*z, clamped [0,100], computed against the same season's data/raw/{season}_combine_results.json cohort (docs/athletic-score-normalization-audit.md:7-11)
athletic_testing.rounding_normalization: cohort-relative (same-season combine class); not commensurable across draft classes per the audit doc
athletic_testing.cohort_dependency: the entire same-season combine-cohort population is a required input; confidence/confidence_band is the one family that is genuinely per-row rather than a fixed constant
athletic_testing.missing_evidence: the archived combine testing-event record(s) with a genuine public-release date, plus the complete pinned same-season cohort population snapshot, plus the pinned z-score/clamp formula citation

college_production.direct_or_derived: NOT a simple pass-through of the file the source_name implies -- copies Rookie Alpha's own scores.production_0_100; 47 of 48 rows differ from data/processed/{season}_college_production.json's own raw production_score_0_100 (examples: 90.0 vs 75.0; 87.6 vs 69.0; 55.94 vs 81.2)
upstream_files: data/processed/{season}_college_production.json (label text only, 48 rows matching population exactly)
college_production.layered_true_pipeline: raw same-season/same-position CFBD z-score (scripts/compute_production_scores.py) blended 0.60/0.40 with a separate age-adjusted score, then WR translation penalties (screen-yard-share, deep-yard-share, slot-contested-rate; up to -8.0 combined) or RB missed-tackle-forced/YAC-consistency penalty (-2.5 to -5)
college_production.join_note: a fuzzy name+school join with an explicit PLAYER_NAME_ALIASES table is used one hop further upstream in compute_production_scores.py; already resolved by the time player_id exists in the file this design inventoried
college_production.derivation_formula_recovered: population construction with per-position eligibility floors (e.g. RB >= 50 carries), per-metric z-scoring, position-weighted composite (RB: 0.45*YPC_z + 0.35*TD_rate_z + 0.20*receiving_yards_z), z_to_score(z) = clamp(0,100, round(50+15z,1))
college_production.rounding_normalization: population/cohort-relative at the raw stage, then blended with an age-adjusted score and position-specific penalty-adjusted
college_production.cohort_dependency: full same-season/same-position eligibility-filtered population snapshot required at the raw stage
college_production.open_cross_family_dependency: UNCERTAIN, flagged not resolved: the age-adjusted blend component appears to depend on the player's age (the age_at_entry family's own value); exact age-adjustment formula not fully recovered in this pass
college_production.current_timestamp_fields: provenance.last_verified_at = generator run date, always; notes is null
college_production.missing_evidence: archived raw per-player CFBD stat record(s), archived population snapshot, pinned raw z-score formula, the under-specified age-adjustment formula and its inputs, pinned WR/RB penalty formulas

official_postdraft_outcome.mirror_value_shape_drafted: { status: drafted, nfl_team, draft_round, overall_pick, is_udfa: false, source_status: external_verified, upstream_provenance_status }
official_postdraft_outcome.mirror_value_shape_udfa: { status: udfa_signed, nfl_team, draft_round: null, overall_pick: null, is_udfa: true, source_status: external_verified, upstream_provenance_status: null }
official_postdraft_outcome.direct_or_derived: priority-ordered lookup (draft_results.json checked first, requires source_status == external_verified; falls back to day3_udfa_draft_result_profiles.json, same gate); status/is_udfa read from the row's own fields, never hard-coded
upstream_files: data/processed/{season}_draft_results.json (81 rows, one per player_id; traces to TIBER-Data's nfl_draft_results v1 contract)
data/processed/{season}_day3_udfa_draft_result_profiles.json (8 rows, an explicitly narrow gap-filler subset)
official_postdraft_outcome.coverage_in_promoted_artifact: 47 drafted / 1 udfa_signed / 0 unavailable across the 48 mirrored players
official_postdraft_outcome.data_quality_finding: one player's player_name is spelled two different ways across two upstream files ('Jeremiah Love' vs 'Jeremiyah Love'); the join still resolves correctly because it is keyed on player_id, not name -- direct evidence a name-based join would silently fail here
official_postdraft_outcome.derivation_code_path: build_official_postdraft_outcome_field()/_postdraft_outcome_from_row(), scripts/compute_rookie_transition_profile.py:261-329; confidence is a fixed constant (0.95) for the whole family, documented as 'a deliberate simplification for v0, not a discovered fact'
official_postdraft_outcome.current_timestamp_fields: drafted path: last_verified_at = source row's own ingested_at (an ingestion/pipeline stamp traced to TIBER-Data's nfl_draft_results generated_at), falling back to generator run date. udfa path: last_verified_at explicitly null, no timestamp field of any kind exists in the source file
official_postdraft_outcome.genuine_public_date_location: only as unstructured free text inside provenance.source_name for the drafted path (e.g. 'published 2026-04-25'), three weeks before the ingestion stamp; absent entirely for the udfa path
official_postdraft_outcome.missing_evidence: an actual archived copy of the cited public source (draft tracker page, team press release) with a machine-extractable publish date and a deterministic record locator naming this specific player

source_inventory.timestamps_cross_cutting_conclusion: Every last_verified_at value in the current pipeline is either the generator's own run date or an ingestion/pipeline timestamp one hop upstream. No field anywhere in the six upstream files or the mirror itself is a genuine, structured public-release timestamp for any family. This is the empirical confirmation of the gap PR #161's hard-rejection decision was based on.
proxy_placeholder_callouts: draft_capital documented as a temporary pre-draft proxy, 'explicitly not equivalent to realized NFL draft capital' (docs/export-contract.md:152-154)
NEUTRAL_DEFAULT (fixed 50.0 athletic-score placeholder) explicitly called 'not a measurement' and deliberately excluded
athletic_score_0_100 documented as potentially misleadingly named -- 'not the Kent Lee Platte RAS percentile most readers would assume' (docs/athletic-score-normalization-audit.md:7-9)
upstream draft_capital_proxy_source free-text field explicitly distrusted in code ('has been found to drift from the actual data')
fixed non-per-row confidence constants for draft_capital (0.65), college_production (0.85), official_postdraft_outcome (0.95) documented as 'a deliberate simplification for v0, not a discovered fact'

a route-level college-production sub-family excluded from v0 because most backing files could not be verified as genuinely CFBD-observed vs manually estimated (docs/rookie-transition-profile-v0-design.md:312)

draft_schedule_data_check.result: confirmed absent in both TIBER-Rookies and TIBER-Data
draft_schedule_data_check.detail: no schedule/start-time/broadcast-time field found in either repo; TIBER-Data's nfl_draft_results v1 contract fields are draft_year/player_id/player_name/position/team/round/pick_in_round/overall_pick/source/source_url/generated_at/provenance_status -- no time-of-day, schedule, or broadcast field
draft_schedule_data_check.consequence: the cutoff contract must be evidenced entirely from an external, independently archived source, not from anything in either repo

artifact_contract_v2.row_population: 48 locked identities x 5 field families = 240 rows; zero/multiple/missing/extra matches fail closed

row_field_notes.mirror_value_literal: cross-checked by the validator against the real pinned mirror value, never trusted from the row alone (schema 1.0.0's proactive self-certification fix, carried forward)
row_field_notes.evidence_package_ids: fixed after review (evidence_package_ids/blocking_reason reconciliation): empty and blocking_reason null for unavailable rows (nothing to evidence); MAY be non-empty for unresolved_no_availability_proof rows to reference attempted-but-rejected packages, paired with a required non-null blocking_reason (empty is also valid here, meaning no attempt was ever made -- schema 1.0.0's baseline case); must be non-empty with blocking_reason null for validator-accepted eligible_at_cutoff/ineligible_after_cutoff rows
row_field_notes.blocking_reason: closed enum recorded whenever evidence was attempted but the row still resolves to unresolved_no_availability_proof -- see status_computation.blocking_reason_enum

artifact_contract_v2.mirror_source: reuses schema 1.0.0's pinned-commit dereferencing contract unchanged (git show/git ls-tree, never the live worktree; source_lock including commit, forecast_mirror.paths, mirrored_hashes all recomputed)

evidence_package_contract.commit_format: full 40-character lowercase hex SHA; never a mutable ref

json_array_exact_match.use_case: one row in a JSON array by exact field equality -- the shape of all six current TIBER-Rookies processed files
json_array_exact_match.cardinality: exactly 1 match required; 0 or >=2 fails closed

json_object_key.use_case: one value by exact string key within a JSON object (not an array)
json_object_key.cardinality: key must exist exactly once; absent key fails closed

json_pointer.use_case: a fixed RFC 6901 pointer directly to one value, only for frozen exact-commit archives
json_pointer.cardinality: must resolve to exactly one value; any failure fails closed

csv_primary_key_match.use_case: one row in a CSV file by exact column match
csv_primary_key_match.cardinality: exactly 1 matching row required; 0 or >=2 fails closed

governed_text_record_range.use_case: unstructured HTML/text (press releases, draft-tracker pages) -- the only locator usable for official_postdraft_outcome's real evidence today
required_fields: extraction_selector (deterministic CSS selector/XPath/byte range against the archived snapshot)
governed_text_record_range.cardinality: selector must resolve to exactly the archived extracted_text, hash-verified; reviewer transcription alone insufficient

evidence_package_contract.record_canonicalization_and_hash: every locator requires a deterministic canonicalization rule (e.g. sorted-key json.dumps) and a SHA-256 record hash, making the located record itself an immutable, independently verifiable fact
evidence_package_contract.ocr_derived_evidence: not authorized under any locator in schema 2.0.0

derivation_contracts.citation_style: pinned by (repo, commit, path, function reference, sha256) rather than transcribed formula text, so the design never drifts from the real code

fully_recovered_and_pinnable_now.age_at_entry: age_from_dob(dob, season): season_start = date(season,9,1); age = season_start.year - born.year, minus 1 if (season_start.month, season_start.day) < (born.month, born.day); cited to scripts/compute_rookie_transition_profile.py::age_from_dob (== scripts/compute_breakout_age.py::age_from_dob)
fully_recovered_and_pinnable_now.draft_capital: DRAFT_CAPITAL_RANK_BANDS: (1,10)->95, (11,20)->85, (21,32)->75, (33,50)->65, (51,75)->55, (76,100)->45, (101,150)->35, (151,inf)->25; cited to scripts/compute_rookie_transition_profile.py::expected_band_score; open item: 101-150/151+ bands undocumented outside code, resolve before implementation

citation_only_pending_full_recovery.athletic_testing: within-class z-score 50+16.6667*z clamped [0,100]; owner scripts/compute_rookie_alpha.py -- exact commit/function pin deferred to implementation issue
citation_only_pending_full_recovery.college_production: raw z-score composite + 0.60/0.40 age-adjusted blend + WR/RB penalty adjustments; owner scripts/compute_rookie_alpha.py and scripts/compute_production_scores.py -- exact coefficients and the age_at_entry cross-family dependency deferred to implementation issue, deliberately not transcribed here to avoid governance-doc drift from the real code

closed_methods.when_usable: the archived record itself carries a player_id/equivalent field byte-for-byte equal to source_identity.source_player_id

the archived record carries a different stable identifier proven equal via a separately governed, TIBER-Rookies-owned crosswalk (not Lane A's Forecast-side GSIS crosswalk); no such crosswalk currently found to exist for any of the six inventoried files -- flagged as an unfilled gap
closed_methods.proves: identity via an independently governed alias mapping

closed_methods.when_usable: the archived record identifies its subject only by name/team/position/school (the common case for real external evidence)
closed_methods.proves: a governed, attributable, one-time human identity decision, cited immutably

subject_binding.grounding_finding: one real player's player_name is spelled two different ways across two TIBER-Rookies upstream files; the join only works because it is player_id-based, not name-based -- direct evidence for why name-only binding is prohibited outright

availability_time_semantics.grounding_finding: every structured timestamp in the current pipeline is exactly one of these prohibited kinds (last_verified_at, ingested_at) -- confirming no existing field could ever satisfy available_at under this contract
required_assertion_fields: record locator or source-field selector (must resolve from the SAME record as the bound value)
machine-verifiable proof the timestamp belongs to the same record as the bound value and subject
availability_time_semantics.available_at_computation: computed by the validator from the evidence assertion(s); never accepted as an independent author-supplied claim
availability_time_semantics.bare_dates: no midnight-in-assumed-timezone manufacturing; schema 2.0.0 does not fully design interval semantics for this case -- prefer unresolved_no_availability_proof until interval handling is designed separately

cutoff_contract.evidence_source: must be an independent external archive (e.g. league draft-day schedule announcement); no draft-schedule data exists in TIBER-Rookies or TIBER-Data
required_bound_facts: event = NFL Draft Day 1 / Round 1 start
cutoff_contract.recomputation_requirement: the artifact may store the computed cutoff_at, but the validator must independently recompute it and reject disagreement
cutoff_contract.human_review_requirement: attributable human sign-off required after machine validation; review cannot substitute for archive-and-record proof

rules: mirror value is present and no complete accepted evidence package exists -> unresolved_no_availability_proof
mirror value is present and complete accepted evidence exists and computed_available_at < computed_cutoff_at -> eligible_at_cutoff
mirror value is present and complete accepted evidence exists and computed_available_at >= computed_cutoff_at -> ineligible_after_cutoff
status_computation.official_postdraft_outcome_restriction: may never resolve to eligible_at_cutoff; complete evidence yields only ineligible_after_cutoff, incomplete yields unresolved_no_availability_proof
status_computation.fail_closed_behavior: contradictory, incomplete, or multiple competing evidence packages fail closed to unresolved_no_availability_proof with an explicit blocking reason; the validator never selects a best timestamp among disagreeing candidates

blocking_reason_enum.fixed_after_review: the original draft required evidence_package_ids empty for every unresolved_no_availability_proof row, making it impossible to record why an attempted package was rejected; unresolved rows may now reference attempted-but-rejected packages, paired with one of these closed reasons
blocking_reason_enum.null_when: evidence_package_ids is empty (nothing attempted), or the row is unavailable, or the row is eligible_at_cutoff/ineligible_after_cutoff (nothing blocked it)

status_computation.computed_available_at_rule: max(all required source-record public-availability timestamps, all required derivation-contract availability timestamps, all required cohort/context snapshot availability timestamps); every input actually used to reproduce the exact mirrored value must be included

computed_available_at_by_family.age_at_entry: max(DOB record, reference-date contract, age-formula contract)
computed_available_at_by_family.athletic_testing: max(testing record, cohort-population snapshot, z-score formula contract)
computed_available_at_by_family.college_production: max(raw stat record(s), population snapshot, raw-formula contract, age-adjustment inputs [including age_at_entry's own computed_available_at if confirmed dependent], blend-weight contract, penalty-formula contract)
computed_available_at_by_family.official_postdraft_outcome: the single bound outcome record's public-release time (no derivation contract; fact carry-through, not a computed score)

draft_capital.validator_recomputes: draft_capital_proxy_0_100 from the archived rank and pinned band formula
draft_capital.hypothetical_valid_example: governed_text_record_range locator over an archived big-board publication page, extracting a hash-pinned substring naming the rank, bound via pinned_governed_alias_artifact, with source_record_published_at from the same page's dateline
draft_capital.hypothetical_invalid_example: correct big_board_rank from one page, but a public-release date sourced from a different page about a different player's ranking -- fails closed on cross-record binding

requires: source_record_published_at (or equivalent) for the DOB record
age_at_entry.validator_recomputes: the mirrored age from the archived DOB and pinned formula
age_at_entry.leap_day_handling: ordinary calendar-date arithmetic; no special case needed since comparison is on (month, day) tuples
age_at_entry.hypothetical_valid_example: governed_text_record_range locator over an archived official combine bio page stating a DOB for the bound player, source_record_published_at from the same page
age_at_entry.hypothetical_invalid_example: DOB correctly extracted from one player's bio, but available_at copied from a different player's bio release date -- fails closed

requires: exact player-specific testing record and all raw measurements
complete same-season cohort/context snapshot if normalized
athletic_testing.hypothetical_valid_example: json_array_exact_match locator over an archived official combine results export naming exact measurements for the bound player, plus a second package for the archived same-season cohort snapshot
athletic_testing.hypothetical_invalid_example: correct measurements for this player, but a different season's cohort snapshot used for the z-score -- fails closed on cohort mismatch

college_production.population_dependency: if the score depends on a full population distribution, the entire pinned population snapshot is part of the evidence contract
college_production.hypothetical_valid_example: json_array_exact_match locator over an archived official CFBD season-stats export for the bound player, plus a separate package for the archived same-season/same-position population snapshot, plus derivation-contract citations
college_production.hypothetical_invalid_example: correct raw per-player stats, but a population snapshot drawn from a different position group -- fails closed on cohort mismatch

official_postdraft_outcome.status_restriction: always post-cutoff; can only become ineligible_after_cutoff or unresolved_no_availability_proof, never eligible_at_cutoff
official_postdraft_outcome.prohibited_timestamps: ingested_at, last_verified_at, or any later mirror-generation timestamp is never the official outcome's availability time
official_postdraft_outcome.hypothetical_valid_example: governed_text_record_range locator over an archived team press-release page naming the bound player as a signed UDFA, source_record_published_at from the same page
official_postdraft_outcome.hypothetical_invalid_example: the correct outcome record for one player, but an availability timestamp extracted from a different team's unrelated announcement page -- fails closed

human_review_contract.canonical_object: one row-level review object, not duplicated across the row and each evidence package

required_future_validator_tests: value and timestamp in different records
correct player but wrong field family
cutoff record is not Day 1 / Round 1 start
row status disagrees with recomputed status
hard_boundaries_not_done_by_this_pr: modify the current 1.0.0 validator or CLI
populate any eligible or ineligible row
evaluate MAE, RMSE, calibration, or predictive usefulness
authorize runtime, production, downstream, or UI use
decision_rationale: The required source-and-derivation inventory is complete for draft_capital and age_at_entry (fully pinnable now) and official_postdraft_outcome (structurally fully understood, evidence-source gap remaining), and partially complete for athletic_testing and college_production (real cohort-relative shape confirmed; exact formula coefficients and one cross-family dependency explicitly deferred to the implementation issue rather than guessed). Every family has a proposed record-bound, subject-bound, semantically-typed evidence contract; the cutoff, common archive/locator/subject-binding/availability-time contracts, status-computation rules, human-review contract, migration rules, and negative-test matrix are all specified.
decision_authorizes: a separate implementation issue for schema 2.0.0 and its validator
